question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a ContractFunctionResult containing crafted log entries with arbitrary topic0/topic1/topic2/topic3 values, causing the stored ContractLog records to contain attacker-controlled event topics that mislead event-subscription clients monitoring specific contract events?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a ContractFunctionResult containing a large number of log entries, causing processContractLogs() to insert an unbounded number of ContractLog records, exhausting database storage and causing a denial-of-service against the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction whose ContractFunctionResult log entries reference a contractId different from the executing contract, causing processContractLogs() to store ContractLog records attributed to an arbitrary contract, poisoning that contract's event history?
